false_decoy.reality: It IS grouped with those three, but not because they're seasons—because they hide drinks (WINE, RUM, ALE, TEA)
false_decoy.setup: Looks like an obvious season with SPRING, SUMMER, AUTUMN
false_decoy.why_it_works: The seasons create a surface-level trap that makes solvers dismiss the group too early or pick it for the wrong reason

thinking.all_16_words: WINTER SPRING SUMMER AUTUMN COAST CLEAR SHOT WILD PIPE HOSE FAUCET NOSE COKE DIESEL PROPANE BUTANE
thinking.decoy_architecture: SPRING pulls between seasons and things that verb (springs leak). COAST pulls between geography and idiom completion (coast is clear). COKE pulls between drinks-inside and taxonomy fuels.

mechanic_balance.cooldown_check: PASS - all chosen mechanics are available
mechanic_balance.last_21_analysis: Only puzzle #137 is tagged. It used: FOUND_IN_SCENE (T1), METAPHOR_SUBSTITUTES (T2), WAYS_TO_VERB (T2), SUFFIX_BLANK (T1). Tier 1 was doubled.
mechanic_balance.warmup_strategy: Avoid doubling Tier 1. Lean toward Tier 2 and introduce Tier 3.

thinking.spine_selection: HIDDEN_WORD_INSIDE (Tier 3) - hidden drinks. This is underused and creates strong misdirection.
thinking.surface_pattern_test: Seasons look obvious but SPRING is a decoy. Fuels are visible but COKE hides COKE→Coke. Leaking things are scattered. Idiom needs assembly.
thinking.theme_repeat_check: PASS - no seasons, leaking things, fuels, or '___ is ___' idioms in last 60 days
thinking.tricked_but_fair: Yes - SPRING genuinely belongs to leaking things but reads as a season. COKE reads as fuel/drug slang but belongs to hidden drinks. COAST could be geography or idiom.
